title: Example Notebook
text:
print("Hello, world!")

Hello, world!



import sys

# Check if git, docker, and docker-compose are installed
git_check = !git --version
docker_check = !docker --version
docker_compose_check = !docker-compose --version
docker_running_check = !docker info > /dev/null 2>&1 && echo "Docker daemon is running" || echo "Docker daemon is not running"

# Check if any software is missing or Docker daemon is not running
if not git_check:
    print("Error: Git is not installed.")
    sys.exit("Exiting: Git is required.")
elif not docker_check:
    print("Error: Docker is not installed.")
    sys.exit("Exiting: Docker is required.")
elif not docker_compose_check:
    print("Error: Docker Compose is not installed.")
    sys.exit("Exiting: Docker Compose is required.")
elif docker_running_check[0] != "Docker daemon is running":
    print("Error: Docker daemon is not running.")
    sys.exit("Exiting: Docker daemon needs to be running.")

# If everything is okay
print("All required software is installed and Docker daemon is running.")

All required software is installed and Docker daemon is running.



!git clone git@github.com:florianbuetow/arxiv_publications_tracker.git
%cd arxiv_publications_tracker
!bash build_and_run.sh > /dev/null 2>&1

# Iterate over all subdiretories in arxiv_publications_tracker/data
import os
for subdir, dirs, files in os.walk("data"):
    for file in files:
        filename = os.path.join(subdir, file)
        if filename.endswith(".json"):
            print(filename)

title: Getting Started with AI Engineering
text: Welcome to my first blog post! In this article, I’ll introduce you to AI Engineering and share what you can expect from this blog.

title: Getting Started with AI Engineering
text:
What is AI Engineering?

AI Engineering combines software engineering principles with machine learning and artificial intelligence to build robust, scalable AI systems.

title: Getting Started with AI Engineering
text:
